nameMeanings.name: Podolsko
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Galima, Gennady, Grigory, Lubov, Mikhail, Milya, Nonna, Pasha, Svetlana, Yevgeny, Yevsey.  Jewish Gerson, Hyman, Yakov.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) and Ukrainian; Slovak and Czech (Podolský): habitational name for someone from Podolia in Ukraine (a region which had a large Jewish population from the Middle Ages up to the Second World War; it is named Podolye in Yiddish), or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous other places called Podole. Compare Podolski .

variants.name: Podolski
meanings: Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Podolia in Ukraine (named Podole in Polish, Podolye in Yiddish), or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous other places called Podole. See also   Podolsky  .
In some cases this may also be a variant transliteration of Ukrainian   Podolsky  , a cognate of 1 above.

historicEvents.description: Poland's capital city is moved from Krakow to Warsaw.

historicEvents.title: Second Northern War
historicEvents.description: "The Second Northern War was a large conflict that involved the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Sweden, Moscow Tsardom, Habsburg Monarchy, Brandernburg-Prussia, and Denmark-Norway. Poland was especially impacted by the ""Swedish Deluge,"" a devastating invasion that resulted in the complete destruction of Warsaw. The war ended with the Treaty of Oliva in 1660, which caused Poland to lose some of their northern territory."
